observations.recommendation:
a. The designated Supply Officer and the Municipal Accountant maintain stock cards and supply ledger cards respectively to support the proper recording and reporting of inventories in the books and be used as basis in the reconciliation of inventory balances with the physical count reports.
b. The designated Supply Officer, together with an Inventory Committee that should be created by the Municipal Mayor, conduct physical count of inventories and prepare reports thereof in accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS for LGUs.

a. The Municipal Treasurer issue warrants of levy and conduct public sale or auction of delinquent real properties. These are indispensable actions to avoid the criminal and administrative penalties provided in RA No. 7160. Coordinate with other local treasurers who had recently conducted public sale or auction in their own local government units to adopt their procedures and best practices.
b. Management provide support to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer in the conduct of public sale or auction. Include in the Municipality’s appropriations the budgetary requirements for the conduct of public sale or auction. Provide augmentation of personnel when needed.

a. The Municipal Mayor as Head of the Procuring Entity, upon determination that there is a valid ground for any other action against the contractor, send a Notice of Termination to the contractor in accordance with the guidelines provided in Annex “I” of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. This is without prejudice to the right of the contractor to due process or to immediately complete the works so that such Notice of Termination will be rescinded.

a. The Municipal Accountant maintain special accounts in the general fund for the Municipality’s public utilities and other economic enterprises and prepare their financial statements at yearend.
b. The Chief of Hospital of Sebaste Community Hospital and the Supervising Public Utilities Regulation Officer extend assistance to the Municipal Accountant in the determination of beginning balances and reconciliation of accounts of the public utilities and other economic enterprises of the Municipality to facilitate the timely recording and reporting of local economic enterprise accounts in the books.

a. The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator set up and maintain a GAD database containing complete data requirements enumerated in Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
b. Management provide budgetary and technical support to the MPDO in the setting up and maintenance of a GAD database.
c. The GAD Focal Point System – Technical Working Group use sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information provided by the GAD database in identifying gender issues and gaps and their related projects and activities for inclusion in the gender-responsive GPB.
We recommended that Management enforce the settlement of the disallowances amounting to P241,988.00 in compliance to COA Circular No. 2009-006 and COA Memorandum No. 2009-084.

We recommended that the amount of P1,477,047.56 be withheld from the claim of Culasi General Merchandising Construction Services if full payment is not yet made otherwise require the contractor and all other persons responsible/liable for the loss of government funds to refund the amount.

We recommended that management should exert effort in securing the records of the Accounts Receivable which remain outstanding for more than ten years and enforce its collection. In the event that its collections are improbable, procedures prescribed pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997 should be followed.

The municipality should intensify collection of the long outstanding loan receivables by sending demand letter to the defaulting debtors and comakers. Initiate legal action against the borrowers/comakers as provided in the Memorandum of Agreement.

1.9 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Regularly coordinate with the implementing offices such as the Mayor’s
Office, Engineering Office or the Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management (DRRM) Office to monitor all on-going and completed
infrastructure projects for proper recording in the books;
b. Prepare and maintain the subsidiary ledgers for all PPE accounts in
order to provide information on the acquisition or construction cost,
additions, disposals and other useful information concerning the PPE
accounts;
c. Make necessary adjustments to reclassify the completed infrastructure
projects to their appropriate PPE accounts, using the following pro-
forma entry; and
Account Debit Credit
Appropriate PPE account xxx
Construction in Progress xxx
To record the reclassification of completed infrastructure projects to their
appropriate PPE accounts.
d. Compute for the depreciation of the reclassified accounts to start from
the date of completion up to the reporting date, using the following pro-
forma entry:
Account Debit Credit
Depreciation Expense (current) xxx
Prior Period Adjustments (prior years) xxx
Accumulated Depreciation xxx
To record the depreciation of the reclassified accounts.

2.10 We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
a. The Municipal Assessor prepare and submit to the Municipal
Treasurer, on or before December 31 of each year, the assessment roll
containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly
assessed or reassessed, along with the corresponding assessed values of
such properties;
b. The Municipal Treasurer then update the Real Property Tax Account
Register (RPTAR)/Taxpayer’s Index Card based on the latest/updated
assessment roll provided by the Municipal Assessor and provide the
Municipal Accountant the computation of tax collectibles for the year;
c. The Municipal Accountant draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to
record the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based
on the information provided by the Municipal Treasurer on the amount
due and collectible for the year, using the following pro-forma entry;
and
Account Debit Credit
Real Property Tax Receivable xxx
Special Education Tax Receivable xxx
Real Property Tax - Basic xxx
Special Education Tax xxx
To record the set-up of receivables at the beginning of the year.
d. The Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments in the
books to correct the reported balances of RPT and SET Receivables as
at reporting period.

3.16 We reiterated the following recommendations and Management agreed
that:
a. The Property Officer and the Municipal Accountant maintain property
cards and property ledger cards, respectively, and update them
periodically to aid the conduct of physical count of PPE;
b. The Inventory Committee conduct a complete physical count of all PPE
excluding CIP annually and prepare the required reports thereof;
c. The Inventory Committee see to it that only tangible items with costs of
with costs of ₱50,000.00 and above are included as PPE in compliance
with COA Circular No. 2024-006;
d. Thereafter, the Property Officer and the Municipal Accountant
reconcile the physical count reports with the balances of PPE accounts
per books to establish the correct balances that should be presented in
the financial statements; and
e. Management avail of the opportunity provided by the COA for the one-
time cleansing of PPE account balances by following the procedures and
guidelines set in COA Circular 2020-006.

4.8 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant:
a. Prospectively, prepare journal entry upon utilization or disbursement
of funds, using the following pro-forma entry; and
Account Debit Credit
Appropriate PPE account (Capital Expenditure) or xxx
Appropriate Expense account (MOOE) xxx
Due to BIR xxx
Cash in Bank xxx
To record the disbursement of funds intended to the purpose of the fund
transfer.
b. Upon submission of liquidation reports to the Source Agency and
fulfilment of the purpose of fund transfer, the following journal entry
shall be made using the Journal Entry Voucher, as follows:
Account Debit Credit
Due to NGAs xxx
Subsidy from National Government xxx
To record the liquidation of received funds.

5.7 We recommended and Management agreed that the Acting Municipal
Treasurer:
a. Establish physical controls over the release of accountable forms such as
keeping the forms in a vault/receptacle/cabinet with lock and use of the
CCTV;
b. Regularly review the correctness and completeness of reporting of
accountable forms as appearing in the RCDs and RAAFs of the
accountable officers and reconcile them with the accountable forms
record book; and
c. Ensure that only sufficient quantities of accountable forms are released
to accountable officers based on their actual needs and no releases be
made if previous collections remain unremitted.

a. The Municipal Budget Officer see to it that the computation of the allowable CF be strictly based on the budget of the Municipality’s POP only.
b. The Municipal Accountant ensure that the amount of confidential expenses will not exceed the authorized funds provided for the CF.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, see to it that projects to be implemented out of the grants, donations and assistances from the national government, other government agencies or the private sector, funds for which have validity or expiration periods, should be prioritized, strictly monitored and implemented timely and in accordance with the agreed terms and conditions and/or guidelines to avoid reversion of funds.

We recommended that the Management of the Municipal School Board avoid the purchase of high-end and expensive information technology equipment, being an “unnecessary” expenditure, out of the SEF unless justified by circumstances.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer prepare and maintain a Cashbook for Cash in Bank for all funds and bank accounts in the required form pursuant to the requirements and instructions provided in the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor as the head of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, with the assistance of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer II, strictly see to it that the LDRRMF is utilized solely for DRRM-related projects and activities and for the purchase of allowed equipment and equipage provided in NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 and DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-73.

We recommended that Management conduct benchmarking activities just within the locality/region with the right size of participants. Conduct only study tours when they are very necessary, with authorization from the DILG and if they are an integral part of the Municipality’s capacity development program.

a. The Municipal Administrator take over the duty of countersigning checks in settlement of the Municipality’s executive branch’s obligations from the Municipal Mayor. The next in rank second-level permanent staff/employee in the Office of the Municipal Administrator take over the countersigning of checks during the Municipal Administrator’s temporary absence or incapacity.
b. The Municipal Treasurer assist in making representation with the Municipality’s depository banks that the Municipal Administrator and his next-in-rank/OIC are the official signatories of the Municipality’s checks and in the taking of their specimen signatures.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer immediately submit all the unsubmitted check disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant who, in turn, will submit the same to the Office of the Auditor, for audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the head of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, with the assistance of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer II, strictly see to it that the LDRRMF is utilized solely for DRRM-related projects and activities provided in NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1.

We recommended that the Management demand the concerned officials and employees to liquidate and settle their cash advances outstanding in the books. Once liquidation has been made, proper adjustments should be made on both the particular expense and the asset accounts. Furthermore, refrain from granting additional cash advances unless the previous ones are first liquidated.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management take the following courses of action:

Require the Property Officer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain property cards and property ledger cards respectively and update them periodically to aid the conduct of physical count of PPE.



Conduct a complete physical count of all PPE excluding CIP annually and prepare the required reports thereof.




Thereafter, reconcile the physical count reports with the balances of PPE accounts per books to establish the correct balances that should be presented in the financial statements.



Avail of the opportunity provided by the COA for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances by following the procedures and guidelines set in COA Circular 2020-006.

We reiterated the following recommendations:

The Municipal Accountant record inventories in the books in accordance with recognition and measurement standards provided in IPSAS 12 – Inventories.


The designated Supply Officer and the Municipal Accountant maintain stock cards and supply ledger cards respectively to support the proper recording and reporting of inventories in the books and be used as basis in the reconciliation of inventory balances with the physical count reports.


The designated Supply Officer, together with an Inventory Committee that should be created by the Municipal Mayor, conduct physical count of inventories and prepare reports thereof in accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer immediately submit all the unsubmitted check disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant who, in turn, will submit the same to the Office of the Auditor, for audit.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Treasurer exert more effort to improve the efficiency of collection of real property taxes. Avail of all collection remedies applicable in the current circumstance of the Municipality.


The Municipal Treasurer issue warrants of levy and conduct public sale or auction of delinquent real properties. These are indispensable actions to avoid the criminal and administrative penalties provided in RA No. 7160. Coordinate with other local treasurers who had recently conducted public sale or auction in their own local government units to adopt their procedures and best practices.

Management provide support to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer in the conduct of public sale or auction. Include in the Municipality’s appropriations the budgetary requirements of the activity and provide augmentation of personnel when needed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the head of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, with the assistance of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer II, strictly see to it that the LDRRMF is utilized solely for DRRM-related projects and activities provided in NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Mayor as Head of the Procuring Entity, upon determination that there is a valid ground for termination of contract, send a Notice of Termination to the contractor in accordance with the guidelines provided in Annex “I” of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. This is without prejudice to the right of the contractor to due process or to immediately complete the works so that such Notice of Termination will be rescinded.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Accountant maintain special accounts in the general fund for the Municipality’s public utilities and other economic enterprises and prepare their financial statements at yearend.



The Chief of Hospital of Sebaste Community Hospital and the Supervising Public Utilities Regulation Officer extend assistance to the Municipal Accountant in the determination of beginning balances and reconciliation of accounts of the public utilities and other economic enterprises of the Municipality to facilitate the timely recording and reporting of local economic enterprise accounts in the books.

We recommended that:

The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator set up and maintain a GAD database containing complete data requirements enumerated in Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. 

Management provide budgetary and technical support to the MPDO in the setting up and maintenance of a GAD database.


The GAD Focal Point System – Technical Working Group use sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information provided by the GAD database in identifying gender issues and gaps and their related projects and activities for inclusion in the gender-responsive GPB.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer regularly update her Cash in Bank cashbooks and reconcile it with the accounting records as required in Section 63 of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

We recommended that the Management demand the concerned officials and employees to liquidate and settle their respective cash advances outstanding in the books. Once liquidation has been made, proper adjustments should be made on both the particular expense and the asset accounts. Furthermore, refrain from granting additional cash advances unless the previous ones are first liquidated.
We recommended that Management enforce the settlement of the disallowances amounting to P241,988.00 in compliance to COA Circular No. 2009-006 and COA Memorandum No. 2009-084.

We recommended that the amount of P1,477,047.56 be withheld from the claim of Culasi General Merchandising and Construction Services if full payment is not yet made otherwise require the contractor and all other persons responsible/liable for the loss of government funds to refund the amount.

To avoid loss of government funds, we also recommended that the Engineering Department and other responsible departments of the Municipality of Sebaste, exercise due care in determining the approved budget for the contract for its projects making sure that the ABC is reasonable. Likewise, bonds which maybe included under Item 1 General Requirements should not be considered as pay item since it is included in the Indirect Cost, B.1 – Overhead Expense, B.1.5d Premium on Warranty Bond as per DPWH Department Order No. 72 dated October 5, 2012.

We recommended that management should exert effort in securing the records of the Accounts Receivable which remain outstanding for more than ten years and enforce its collection.  In the event that its collections are improbable, procedures prescribed pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997 should be followed.

The municipality should intensify collection of the long outstanding loan receivables by sending demand letter to the defaulting debtors and comakers. Initiate legal action against the borrowers/comakers as provided in the Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Mayor and the heads of the receiving municipal department/ office inform the Municipal Accountant whenever they receive donations in kind from other government agencies and private individuals so that the property, plant and equipment (PPE) or semi-expendable properties received and their related revenues be properly recognized, measured and recorded in the Municipality's books of accounts.

The Municipal Mayor organize the Municipality's Appraisal Committee which will provide for the fair values of the received PPE or semi-expendable properties at the time of acquisition.

The Municipal Accountant, based on the information and documents provided by the Municipal Mayor and/or the heads of the receiving municipal department/office and the fair values of the received PPE or semi-expendable properties provided by the Appraisal Committee, record the received assets and its related revenues in the Municipality's books of accounts by debiting the Specific PPE or semi-expendable property accounts and crediting the Grants and Donations in Kind account with account number 4-04-02-020. Specifically, for the unrecorded receipt of donations in kind in CY 2022, the Municipal Accountant prepare the following journal entry:

Account Name        Account No.     Debit    Credit

Specific PPE or                               xxx (Fair Value)
semi-expandable 
property accounts
     Prior Period 
     Adjustment        3-01-01-020                   xxx (Fair Value)                  

To record PPE and semi-expendable properties received as donations from other government agencies and private individuals in CY 2022.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator assist the Local Chief Executive in the rationalization of the jobs that need to be carried out in the implementation of the different programs of the Municipality and the rightsizing in the hiring of job order personnel to avoid mismatch in the assignment of work of such personnel and the mischarging of their wages.

The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant, when certifying as to the availability of funds for the purpose and the completeness of supporting documents, respectively, during payments of wages of job order personnel, see to it that the jobs and work accomplishments these personnel perform coincide with and are supportive of the objectives of the office/program from where their wages are charged.

We again commended and congratulated the Municipality and its management for having received awards in CY 2022. We recommended that the Municipality’s officials and employees continuously improve and sustain these local good governance efforts.

We recommended that Management comply with the required submission of contracts and purchase orders together with its supporting documents indicating all the necessary information pertaining to the purchase/contract within five (5) working days as required under COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 96-010 dated February 12, 2009 and August 15, 1996 respectively to facilitate review process.

The Municipal Accountant record inventories in the books in accordance with recognition and measurement standards provided in IPSAS 12 – Inventories.

The designated Supply Officer and the Municipal Accountant maintain stock cards and supply ledger cards respectively to support the proper recording and reporting of inventories in the books and be used as basis in the reconciliation of inventory balances with the physical count reports.

The designated Supply Officer, together with an Inventory Committee that should be created by the Municipal Mayor, conduct physical count of inventories and prepare reports thereof in accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS for LGUs.

The Municipal Engineer submit a written report to the Municipal Mayor stating the extent of unfinished works, delay and slippage in the implementation of the project and his recommendations thereto with due consideration to the regulations provided by the GPPB and the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

The Municipal Mayor as Head of the Procuring Entity, upon determination that there is a valid ground for termination of contract, send a Notice of Termination to the contractor in accordance with the guidelines provided in Annex “I” of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. This is without prejudice to the right of the contractor to due process or to immediately complete the works so that such Notice of Termination will be rescinded.

The Municipal Accountant, based on the report of work accomplishment of the Municipal Engineer, compute for liquidated damages and such amount shall be deducted from future payments to be made to the contractor because the project was not completed within the revised contract expiry date.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the assistance of the Chief of Hospital of the SCH, the Municipal Treasurer, the Assistant Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant, immediately send a demand letter to the PhilHealth and/or institute necessary administrative or legal actions so that funds due the SCH will be rightfully collected.

Ensure that goods be purchased strictly in accordance with the allowed methods of procurement provided in RA No. 9184, its revised IRR and the issuances of the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPBB).

Allow reimbursements to officials and employees who directly purchased goods for use of their offices only on highly exceptional circumstances, e.g., purchase of petroleum, oil and lubricant (POL) products and airline tickets in accordance with the GPBB Resolution No. 24-2019.

Establish a Petty Cash Fund to sufficiently cover the Municipality’s non-recurring, emergency and petty expenses for one month which shall be subject for replenishment and shall be managed by a Petty Cash Custodian.

We commended and congratulated the Municipality and its management for having received the awards. We recommend that the Municipality’s officials and employees continuously improve and sustain these local good governance efforts.

We have already recommended in the previous year that Management cause its settlement but no action was taken. We reiterated our recommendation that Management enforce the settlement of the disallowances in compliance to COA Circular No. 2009-006 and COA Memorandum No. 2009-084.

The Municipal Accountant record expenditures to their appropriate asset or expense accounts considering the nature or purpose of the transactions and in consonance with what is prescribed in the Chart of Accounts embodied in Volume III of the GAM for LGUs. Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses account be used only to record expenses not falling under any specific maintenance and other operating expense accounts.

The Municipal Budget Officer, in the preparation of future executive budgets, see to it that only expenses that could not classify to any other account be provided with budget under the Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses account. Budgetary accounts be unified and harmonized with the accounts used in the recording of financial transactions in the books.

Shopping, as an alternative method of procurement, be resorted to by the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) only when there is an unforeseen contingency requiring immediate purchase or if ordinary or regular office supplies and equipment to be procured are not available in the Procurement Service, and subject to the thresholds prescribed in Annex “H” of the Updated 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

Procurement of goods not covered by Shopping and not exceeding ₱100,000.00, the threshold set in Annex “H” of the Updated 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 for 4th Class Municipalities, may be coursed through Negotiated Procurement – Small Value Procurement. Resort to such alternative method of procurement be reflected in the Municipality’s Annual Procurement Plan (APP) or through a BAC resolution.

The Local Chief Executive:

Cause the termination of the MOA with the supplier on the provision of meals to patients of the Sebaste Community Hospital.

Ensure that meals served to patients of Sebaste Community Hospital be procured in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its Revised implementing rules and regulations (IRR). Contracts resulting thereto be submitted to the Department of Health (DOH) to comply on the requirements of renewal of hospital accreditation instead of the MOA.

As Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) approve the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) recommendation of the most appropriate method of procurement with consideration on the timely and unhampered delivery of meals to patients of Sebaste Community Hospital.

The BAC submit written justification and/or additional documents to support the conduct of re-bidding because of failure of bidding, and resort to negotiated procurement due to two failed biddings in the procurement of the emergency rescue vehicle.

Prospectively, the BAC conduct mandatory review and evaluation of the terms, conditions, and specifications in the Bidding Documents, including its cost estimates before every re-bidding because of failure of bidding, and resort to negotiated procurement due to two failed biddings. We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the   Municipal
      Accountant to comply strictly with COA Circular No.  2009-006  on  the
      submission of accounts and financial reports to facilitate  audit  and
      early submission of the LGU’s Annual Audit Report as required  by  the
      Commission. Furthermore, the Municipal Treasurer observe the deadlines
      of submitting the reports and accounts to the Accounting Office.

We recommended that management  strictly  comply  with  the  reporting
      requirements on the fund utilization pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-
      002 dated September 12, 2012. In particular, the Municipal  Accountant
      should timely prepare the required monthly report and the  same  shall
      be submitted to the Office of the Auditor by the Local  Disaster  Risk
      Reduction Management Office (LDRRMO) on or before the 15th  day  after
      the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council
      (LDC).

We recommended that management submit the duly filled up Agency Action
      Plan and Status of Implementation Form on the audit  observations  and
      recommendations embodied in the CY 2015  AAR  to  the  Office  of  the
      Auditor. All  columns  in  the  form  be  accomplished  including  the
      detailed   action   plan,   person/department   responsible,    target
      implementation date, and status of implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the liquidations of cash advances soon as the liquidation reports and supporting documents have been forwarded to his office. Furthermore, we recommended that the Assistant Municipal Treasurer acting as Disbursing Officer cause the liquidation of all cash advances for salaries, wages, allowances and honoraria whose purpose have been completed or served in accordance with the provision of Section 5.1.1 of COA Circular 97-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit her collections intact daily and strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 32, Volume I of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units and Section 69 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Municipality acquire first the title over the land where various government projects will be constructed. In the planning stage, ownership of the land should be considered so as not to cause delay in the implementation of the project.

We recommend that the Local Accountant prepare and certify the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) thru the LDRRMC and LDC cause its submission to the local auditor monthly.  Moreover, strict compliance of the circular, particularly on its recording, accounting and monitoring  is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: Quarterly reports and accounting of LDRRM Fund were submitted to the Auditors. Reiterated in this year’s report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the strict adherence to Section 7 of RA 9679, which fixes the maximum government/ employer’s share in the monthly contribution to HDMF at P100.00 per employee. It is also recommended to negotiate with the HDMF to apply the excess payments of HDMF premiums to future payments of the LGU’s contribution for its employees.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive assign the Municipal Assessor to process the titles of these properties in the name of the agency to avoid contingent claims of other parties that may arise in the future.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor was assigned to conduct an inventory of all land owned by the Municipality and process the titles of all these lots in the name of the Municipality, however, to date not a single lot was titled.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer should strictly monitor at the close of each business day that all collections are completely remitted by checking the accountable forms daily. The Treasurer/Liquidating Officer should strictly comply with Section 32 of NGAS for LGUs to prevent any untoward incident to happen while so much cash is kept in the vault of the Treasurer’s Office. Compliance with the provisions of Sections 29 and 32 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume 1, should be strictly enforced, to protect government funds from risk of loss and/or misapplication.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is monitoring collections and remittances of collectors, however, no preventive action is taken to avoid increase in unremitted collections

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should direct the implementing office to mobilize the implementation of the projects. The LGU should maximize the utilization of the 20% development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help the attainment of social, economic and environmental development.
observations.managementAction: A review of unimplemented projects is undergoing as well as the status of ongoing and previously implemented projects.  Management assured its full implementation before the year ends.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU consider the remittance of contributions to the respective agencies as priority obligation of the LGU Sebaste so as to avoid penalties and surcharges that may be imposed for late remittances.  Penalties and surcharges cannot be charged against public funds and will be considered as the personal liability of the officer charged of such duty.

We recommend that properly accomplished, serially numbered trip tickets should be prepared for each trip.  Should the odometers of the vehicles are not functioning, cause its repair in order to have accurate readings of distances traveled and approximate quantity of fuel consumption.  Strict compliance on COA Circular No. 77-61 “Prescribing the Use of Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles” is recommended.
observations.managementAction: Gasoline, oil and fuel is regularly monitored, however, completion of required reports is not assured.

observations.recommendation: The municipality should intensify collection of the long outstanding loan receivables by sending demand letter to the defaulting debtors and comakers. Initiate legal action against the borrowers/comakers as provided in the Memorandum of Agreement.
observations.managementAction: Collection letters were sent out, however, payments were very minimal. No other action was taken.
